title: phlegmariurus nesselii
intro: Phlegmariurus nesselii, also known as the Nessel's climbing forked polypody, is a species of club moss in the family Lycopodiaceae. It is a climbing plant with forked branches that is native to tropical regions of the Americas, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, Phlegmariurus nesselii is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health.
